name: Pickled Banana Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the banana peppers in half lengthwise.
2. Rinse and discard the seeds as well as any interior pulp.
3. In a bowl, combine the 4 cups water and the salt. Cover the peppers with the salt solution and let stand overnight. Drain; rinse well.
4. In a large saucepan, combine the 3 cups water, vinegar, basil, and oregano. Bring to boiling. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, about 10 minutes.
5. Pack the prepared peppers into hot clean half-pint jars, leaving 1/2 inch headspace.
6. Add one clove of garlic to each jar.
7. Pour the hot vinegar mixture over the peppers, stirring to keep herbs evenly distributed. Leave 1/2 inch headspace in each jar.
8. Prepare the lids according to manufacturer's directions.
9. Wipe jar rims. Adjust lids and process in boiling water bath for 10 minutes, starting the timer when the water returns to boiling.
